waterTable.evidenceSnippet: No groundwater encountered to 35 feet below grade in Boring B-1.
Dry conditions throughout drilling of Boring B-2 to auger refusal.
No free water observed in Boring B-3 to 30 feet.
Groundwater not encountered to terminal depth in Boring B-4.
Dry drilling conditions throughout Boring B-5.

borings.notes: Stiff to very stiff clay profile. Excellent bearing capacity. Refusal on weathered sandstone.
Uniform conditions with good SPT values throughout. Sandstone at 24 feet.
Very consistent profile. Dense gravel transition zone above bedrock.
Favorable conditions. Moderate to high SPT values increasing with depth.
Good bearing soils. Sandstone at moderate depth suitable for pier bearing.

slab.summary: Conventional slab-on-grade suitable. Minimum 6-inch moisture-conditioned pad of select fill.
Subgrade should be proof-rolled and any soft areas repaired to minimum 95% Standard Proctor density.

foundation.summary: Conventional spread footings recommended with 3,500 psf allowable bearing pressure on native clay at 3 feet depth.
Mat foundation also suitable for the building core with 4,000 psf allowable bearing on dense soils.

pavement.summary: Standard pavement section: 2-inch HMAC surface, 8-inch aggregate base on compacted subgrade.
No special subgrade treatment required. Standard compaction to 95% modified Proctor sufficient.

riskFlags.evidence: Sandstone bedrock at 24-30 feet depth. May require rock excavation for deep utilities.
riskFlags.description: Plan for potential rock excavation in utility corridors exceeding 6 feet depth.

riskFlags.evidence: Minor seasonal moisture variation expected in upper clay soils.
riskFlags.description: Standard moisture conditioning during slab construction should be sufficient.

chatResponses.response: No groundwater was encountered at any boring location. This site has excellent subsurface water conditions for construction.
Conventional spread footings at 3,500 psf are recommended. This is a very straightforward foundation design with excellent bearing soils.
This is a low-risk site (score: 35). Minor concerns include potential rock excavation for deep utilities and standard moisture conditioning.
